expectations.text: Phase 1 asks user to explain Bethe-Bloch Formula in their own words
expectations.evidence: No Feynman check phase. Jumps straight to 16 quiz questions. No 'explain in your own words' prompt.

expectations.text: Phase 1 feedback identifies key omissions (beta-gamma dependence, MIP, corrections)
expectations.evidence: Part 2 evaluation identifies: vague speed vs beta-gamma, missing z² charge dependence, no MIP terminology, no corrections, no applications. Thorough analysis.

expectations.text: Phase 1 generates exactly 3 follow-up probing questions
expectations.evidence: Generates 10 follow-up questions (F1-F10), not 3. No constraint on count.

expectations.text: Phase 2 generates 5 adversarial questions with correct type distribution (2 trick, 2 cross-concept, 1 exam-style)
expectations.evidence: Generates 16 questions with custom categories (4 conceptual, 4 trick, 6 cross-topic, 2 synthesis). Does not follow 2/2/1 distribution.

expectations.text: Cross-concept questions reference actual wikilinks from the Bethe-Bloch Formula note
expectations.evidence: References TPC, ToF, Ionizing Radiation, Particle Identification, Linear Energy Transfer — all from the note's wikilinks
No English language correction section. Baseline has no awareness of the user being a non-native English learner.

summary: Baseline produces high-quality physics content but without the structured phases, question count constraints, or English correction. Overwhelming volume (16+10 questions vs 5+3).
